history: The client has a pattern of relationships sometimes turning romantic, and has experienced conflict in relationships after expressing anger. There is a complex relationship with parents involving obligations, which may contribute to stress. The client demonstrates perfectionistic tendencies in academics, reporting significant stress over minor setbacks. He also describes chronic social anxiety, avoidance of social situations, and a tendency to minimize the emotional impact of family interactions. Substance use (marijuana) and high caffeine intake are used to cope with anxiety and concentration difficulties.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be abandoned
intermediate_belief: If I lose control of my emotions in front of others, they will see me as unstable and distance themselves from me. I must always keep my feelings in check to avoid conflict and rejection. If I don't meet others' expectations, I risk being seen as a failure.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I can't control my emotions or meet expectations, it means I'm fundamentally flawed and nothing I do will be good enough. There's no point in trying because I'll just end up disappointing others and myself.
coping_strategies: The client tends to avoid social situations or withdraw when feeling anxious or after emotional outbursts. He uses marijuana several times a week to relax, despite knowing it can sometimes worsen anxiety. He also relies on caffeine to manage concentration, and sits at the back of the class to facilitate escape from uncomfortable situations.
situation: An incident at a social gathering resulted in an emotional outburst among friends.
auto_thought: I can't believe I lost control in front of everyone. They're all going to think I'm unstable and not want me around.
behavior: I withdrew from the group and avoided talking to anyone after the outburst. I replayed the incident in my head and considered using marijuana to calm down.
